symbols.name: BrokerInfo
symbols.doc:
DeviceInfo defines capabilities for querying device information.
This interface is used to retrieve information related to the OS, hardware, user, and monitors.

methods.name: DeviceId
methods.doc:
DeviceId gets the unique device identifier.
The return value can be used to distinguish device identity.

methods.name: DeviceName
methods.doc:
DeviceName gets the device name.
The return value is usually the device name configured in system settings.

methods.name: GetComputerName
methods.doc:
GetComputerName gets the computer name.
The return value is the hostname or system computer name.

methods.name: GetGpuMemoryMB
methods.doc:
GetGpuMemoryMB gets GPU memory size.
The return value is in MB.

methods.name: GetMonitors
methods.doc:
GetMonitors gets information for all monitors.
The return value is a list of monitor information.

methods.name: GetPrimaryMonitor
methods.doc:
GetPrimaryMonitor gets information for the primary monitor.
The return value is the current system primary monitor.

methods.name: GetUserName
methods.doc:
GetUserName gets the current user name.
The return value is the name of the currently logged-in account.

methods.name: HasNvidiaGPU
methods.doc:
HasNvidiaGPU checks whether an Nvidia GPU exists.
Returns true if an Nvidia graphics card is detected.

methods.name: NumLogicCPU
methods.doc:
NumLogicCPU gets the number of logical CPU cores.
The return value is the number of logical processors.

methods.name: OS
methods.doc:
OS gets the operating system name.
Example return values: Windows, Linux, Darwin.

methods.name: OSVersion
methods.doc:
OSVersion gets the operating system version.
The return value is usually a system version string.

symbols.name: HttpClient
symbols.doc:
HttpClient defines capabilities for HTTP requests and domain-level configuration.
This interface supports domain-level defaults, generic request execution, and common convenience operations.

methods.name: DeleteDomainCookie
methods.doc:
DeleteDomainCookie deletes cookies under the specified domain.
Multiple cookie names can be deleted at once.

methods.name: DeleteDomainHeader
methods.doc:
DeleteDomainHeader deletes headers under the specified domain.
Multiple header names can be deleted at once.

methods.name: DownloadFile
methods.doc:
DownloadFile downloads a file and returns the local path.
The headers argument is used to attach request headers for download.

methods.name: DownloadFileTo
methods.doc:
DownloadFileTo downloads a file to the specified local path.
options controls request timeout and retry behavior.
methods.signature: DownloadFileTo(url string, savePath string, headers map[string]string, options HttpRequestOptions) (string, error)

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get sends an HTTP GET request.
The headers argument is used to override or add request headers.

methods.name: GetDomainCookies
methods.doc:
GetDomainCookies gets cookies for the specified domain.
Returns the saved cookie key-value pairs under that domain.

methods.name: GetDomainHeaders
methods.doc:
GetDomainHeaders gets default headers for the specified domain.
Returns configured request-header key-value pairs under that domain.

methods.name: Post
methods.doc:
Post sends an HTTP POST request with raw data.
The body argument is the request body byte array.
methods.signature: Post(url string, body []byte, headers map[string]string) ([]byte, error)

methods.name: PostForm
methods.doc:
PostForm sends an HTTP form request.
The params argument is submitted in form format.
methods.signature: PostForm(url string, params map[string]any, headers map[string]string) ([]byte, error)

methods.name: PostJson
methods.doc:
PostJson sends an HTTP JSON request.
The params argument is encoded as JSON as the request body.
methods.signature: PostJson(url string, params map[string]any, headers map[string]string) ([]byte, error)

methods.name: Request
methods.doc:
Request sends a generalized HTTP request.
Supports arbitrary HTTP methods and returns structured response metadata.

methods.name: SetDomainCookies
methods.doc:
SetDomainCookies sets cookies for the specified domain.
cookies uses key-value pairs to represent cookie names and values.

methods.name: SetDomainHeaders
methods.doc:
SetDomainHeaders sets default headers for the specified domain.
headers uses key-value pairs to represent request headers.

methods.name: UploadData
methods.doc:
UploadData uploads file data from memory.
fileName is the upload file name, and fileData is the upload content.
methods.signature: UploadData(url string, fileName string, fileData []byte, params map[string]any, headers map[string]string) ([]byte, error)

methods.name: UploadFile
methods.doc:
UploadFile uploads a local file.
filePath is the local file path, and params are additional form parameters.
methods.signature: UploadFile(url string, filePath string, params map[string]any, headers map[string]string) ([]byte, error)

symbols.name: HttpRequest
symbols.doc:
HttpRequest defines a general HTTP request model.
This struct supports arbitrary methods and per-request configuration.

fields.name: Body
fields.doc: Body is the raw request body bytes.

fields.name: Form
fields.doc: Form defines form fields for form submissions.

fields.name: Json
fields.doc: Json defines JSON payload values for JSON requests.

fields.name: Method
fields.doc: Method is the HTTP method, such as GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, or HEAD.

fields.name: Options
fields.doc: Options controls timeout and retry behavior for this request.

fields.name: URL
fields.doc: URL is the target request URL.

symbols.name: HttpRequestOptions
symbols.doc:
HttpRequestOptions defines request-level control options.
This struct is used to configure timeout and retry behavior for a single request.

fields.name: FollowRedirect
fields.doc:
FollowRedirect controls whether redirects should be followed automatically.
If true, redirects are handled by the implementation.

fields.name: RetryCount
fields.doc:
RetryCount defines how many times to retry when transient errors occur.
Zero means no retry.

fields.name: RetryInterval
fields.doc:
RetryInterval defines the interval between retries.
Zero value means using implementation default.

fields.name: Timeout
fields.doc:
Timeout limits the maximum duration of a request.
Zero value means using implementation default.

symbols.name: HttpResponse
symbols.doc:
HttpResponse defines a normalized HTTP response model.
This struct contains status code, headers, and body data.

fields.name: Body
fields.doc: Body is the raw response body bytes.

fields.name: FinalURL
fields.doc: FinalURL is the final URL after redirects.

fields.name: StatusCode
fields.doc: StatusCode is the HTTP status code returned by the server.

symbols.name: LocalFilesystem
symbols.doc:
LocalFilesystem defines capabilities for local filesystem operations.
This interface covers common scenarios such as path handling, file I/O, and directory management.
Path and permission rules:
1) All relative paths are resolved from DataRoot.
2) Files and directories under DataRoot are granted full permissions by default.
3) Files and directories under ScriptRoot are read-only.
4) Paths outside DataRoot and ScriptRoot require explicit authorization by the implementation.

methods.name: CopyFile
methods.doc:
CopyFile copies a file.
src is the source file, and dst is the destination file.
Relative paths are resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: CreateTmpFile
methods.doc:
CreateTmpFile creates a temporary file.
expiredAt indicates the expiration time of the temporary file; returns the file path.

methods.name: DataRoot
methods.doc:
DataRoot gets the root directory for script data storage.
This directory is intended for storing script-generated data and files.
Relative paths in this interface are resolved from this root.

methods.name: IsDir
methods.doc:
IsDir checks whether a path is a directory.
Returns whether it is a directory and any possible error.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: IsFile
methods.doc:
IsFile checks whether a path is a file.
Returns whether it is a file and any possible error.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: JoinPath
methods.doc:
JoinPath joins paths starting from the root directory.
Returns the joined full path string based on DataRoot.

methods.name: ListDir
methods.doc:
ListDir lists entries in a directory.
Returns entry names under the target directory.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: Mkdir
methods.doc:
Mkdir creates a directory.
path is the directory path to create.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: MkdirAll
methods.doc:
MkdirAll creates directories recursively.
If directories already exist, behavior depends on the implementation.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: PathExists
methods.doc:
PathExists checks whether a path exists.
Returns whether it exists and any possible error.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: ReadFile
methods.doc:
ReadFile reads file content.
Returns the read byte data and any possible error.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: Remove
methods.doc:
Remove deletes a file or directory.
path is the target path to delete.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: RemoveAll
methods.doc:
RemoveAll recursively deletes a directory and all its contents.
path is the directory path to delete recursively.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: Rename
methods.doc:
Rename renames or moves a file or directory.
src is the source path, and dst is the destination path.
Relative paths are resolved from DataRoot.

methods.name: ScriptRoot
methods.doc:
ScriptRoot gets the root directory for script files.
This directory is intended for storing script files.
Paths under this root are read-only.

methods.name: StatPath
methods.doc:
StatPath gets metadata for a path.
Returns whether the path exists, whether it is a directory, size in bytes, and last modified time.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.
methods.signature: StatPath(path string) (exists bool, isDir bool, size int64, modifiedAt time.Time, err error)

methods.name: WriteFile
methods.doc:
WriteFile writes content to a file.
data is the byte data to write.
Relative path is resolved from DataRoot.

symbols.name: LocalStorage
symbols.doc:
LocalStorage defines local key-value storage capabilities.
This interface supports single and batch read/write operations, as well as expiration settings.

methods.name: Del
methods.doc:
Del deletes one or more keys.
Returns the number of keys actually deleted.

methods.name: Get
methods.doc:
Get gets the value for the specified key.
If the key does not exist, the return value depends on the implementation.

methods.name: Keys
methods.doc:
Keys gets all keys in the current storage.
Returns a list of key names.

methods.name: MGet
methods.doc:
MGet gets values for multiple keys in batch.
Returns a key-value mapping result.

methods.name: MSet
methods.doc:
MSet sets multiple key-value pairs in batch.
data is the key-value mapping to write.

methods.name: MSetEx
methods.doc:
MSetEx sets multiple key-value pairs with expiration in batch.
expiredAt is the unified expiration timestamp.

methods.name: Set
methods.doc:
Set sets a key-value pair.
key is the key name, and value is the value.

methods.name: SetEx
methods.doc:
SetEx sets a key-value pair with an expiration time.
expiredAt is the expiration timestamp.
methods.signature: SetEx(key string, value string, expiredAt int64)

symbols.name: MonitorInfo
symbols.doc:
MonitorInfo defines capabilities for querying monitor information.
This interface is used to get bounds, work area, DPI, and primary-screen status.

methods.name: GetBounds
methods.doc:
GetBounds gets the monitor bounds position and size.
Returns the full monitor bounds rectangle.

methods.name: GetDPI
methods.doc:
GetDPI gets DPI scaling information of the monitor.
The return value is horizontal DPI or a related scaling value.

methods.name: GetWorkArea
methods.doc:
GetWorkArea gets the monitor work-area position and size.
The work area usually excludes system-occupied areas such as the taskbar.

methods.name: IsPrimary
methods.doc:
IsPrimary indicates whether this is the primary monitor.
Returns true when the current monitor is the primary monitor.

symbols.name: ScriptLogger
symbols.doc:
ScriptLogger defines script runtime logging capabilities.
This interface provides methods for outputting formatted logs by level.

methods.name: Debug
methods.doc:
Debug outputs debug-level logs.
format is the format string, and args are formatting arguments.

methods.name: Error
methods.doc:
Error outputs error-level logs.
format is the format string, and args are formatting arguments.

methods.name: Info
methods.doc:
Info outputs info-level logs.
format is the format string, and args are formatting arguments.

methods.name: Warn
methods.doc:
Warn outputs warning-level logs.
format is the format string, and args are formatting arguments.
